name: Bank 7
description_short: Bank7 is a full-service bank based in Oklahoma City offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services with FDIC insurance protection.
description_long: Bank7 is a traditional commercial bank headquartered in Oklahoma City and publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker BSVN. The company operates as a full-service financial institution serving both personal and business customers through its flagship Bank7 brand. Bank7 Corp., the parent holding company, reports quarterly earnings and declares regular cash dividends to shareholders, indicating a mature, established banking operation. Bank7 offers a comprehensive suite of banking products for personal customers including checking and money market accounts, savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, debit cards, credit cards, personal loans, and home mortgages. On the business side, they provide commercial checking and money market accounts, CDs, commercial loans, agricultural loans, and specialized treasury management services including ACH origination, lockbox services, positive pay, remote deposit capture, and zero balance accounts. The bank also provides wire transfer services, foreign currency exchange, and supports digital payments through Zelle, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Android Pay. Bank7 distinguishes itself through FDIC insurance coverage on deposit accounts (with an online calculator tool called EDIE available to customers), a dedicated online education center for financial literacy, and mobile wallet integration with multiple platforms. They emphasize 24/7 customer service for lost or stolen card reporting and offer emerging technologies like contactless Visa debit cards. The bank explicitly states it does not share, distribute, or sell customer information to third parties, addressing privacy concerns directly on their website. As a traditional bank rather than a fintech or alternative lender, Bank7 is best suited for customers seeking a stable, established financial institution with full-service offerings. The main caveat is that they function as a conventional bank with standard approval requirements and processes, rather than serving customers with urgent cash needs or credit challenges. Their specific lending products (mortgages, personal loans, commercial loans) suggest competitive terms but require meeting traditional underwriting standards.
services: Personal checking and money market accounts
Credit cards with Visa branding and contactless technology
Debit cards with mobile wallet integration (Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, Android Pay)
Commercial checking and money market accounts
Treasury management services including ACH origination, lockbox services, and positive pay
Wire transfer services and foreign currency exchange
Online and mobile banking with Zelle integration
1031 tax-deferred property services for business customers
pros: FDIC insurance protection on deposit accounts with online calculator tool (EDIE estimator) to verify coverage
Comprehensive mobile wallet support including Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Android Pay for secure contactless payments
Free Zelle transfers between U.S. bank accounts through their mobile app
Publicly traded on NASDAQ with transparent quarterly earnings reports and regular cash dividends
Explicit privacy policy stating they do not share, distribute, or sell customer information to third parties
24/7 customer service for lost or stolen debit card reporting via dedicated phone line (833-999-1105)
Specialized business services including agricultural loans, treasury management, ACH origination, and lockbox services
Online education center providing financial literacy resources including identity theft prevention guidance
cons: Website content does not specify interest rates, APRs, fees, or minimum balance requirements for any products
Limited information about loan approval timelines, terms, or qualification criteria for personal loans and mortgages
No mention of mobile app ratings, user experience features, or comparison to competitors' digital offerings
Business account customers must contact local branch to enroll in online banking, creating friction in the digital signup process
No information about branch locations, number of ATMs, or geographic service area despite being a traditional bank
best_for: Small business owners seeking specialized services like agricultural loans, treasury management, and ACH origination
Homebuyers looking for personalized mortgage guidance and home loan products from an established bank
Privacy-conscious consumers who want explicit assurance their financial data will not be sold to third parties
Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts with tools to verify insurance coverage limits
diagnosis: Bank7 is best for individuals and businesses seeking traditional banking services from an established, FDIC-insured institution with comprehensive product offerings and strong privacy protections. The main caveat is that as a conventional bank, they require meeting standard underwriting criteria and do not serve customers needing urgent cash, credit repair, or alternative lending solutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
